articles.title: Gujarat heatwave: Rajkot tops India heat chart at 42 degrees Celsius, Ahmedabad sizzles at 41.4; IMD issue - The Economic Times
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat") Gujarat
articles.full_text:
Gujarat has emerged as one of the hottest regions in India this week, with temperatures soaring well above seasonal averages. According to the national bulletin issued by the India Meteorological Department (IMD), Rajkot recorded a blistering 42°C on Tuesday, making it the highest maximum temperature reported among all IMD weather stations across the country.
Heatwave in Gujarat
The weather office said heatwave to severe heatwave conditions gripped many places in Gujarat's Saurashtra and Kutch regions as several parts of the country, including Maharashtra's Vidarbha region, recorded above-normal maximum temperatures. The IMD said that parts of Gujarat would continue to witness such conditions till March 13."Heatwave to severe heatwave conditions very likely in many/some places over… Saurashtra and Kutch on… March 12, and heatwave conditions in isolated pockets over the Gujarat region on March 12 and March 13, and over Saurashtra and Kutch on March 13," the IMD said.

Ahmedabad weather update
Ahmedabad is witnessing an intense early heat spell as temperatures surge far above seasonal averages. On Wednesday, the city recorded a scorching maximum temperature of 41.4°C, which is 7.2°C higher than the normal level for this time of the year. The minimum temperature also remained unusually high at 22.5°C, about 4.6°C above normal, indicating persistent warm conditions even during the night.The IMD said the heat in Ahmedabad is likely to continue. On Thursday, the maximum temperature is expected to hover around 41°C, while the minimum may settle near 24°C, offering little relief to residents.
IMD officials highlighted that the departure from normal temperatures is particularly alarming. Cities such as Ahmedabad and Rajkot are currently experiencing temperatures nearly 7°C above seasonal averages, a sign of severe heat conditions despite summer only just beginning.
“Apart from the high temperatures, the deviation from normal levels is also significant. In places like Rajkot and Ahmedabad, temperatures are about 7 degrees above normal, indicating severe heat conditions even though the summer season has just started,” an IMD official said.

Gujarat heatwave: Advisory issued
Amid the rising temperatures, officials from the Ahmedabad Municipal Corporation (AMC) and medical experts have issued a public advisory urging residents to take precautions during the hottest hours of the day.Authorities have advised people to avoid stepping out during the afternoon whenever possible, as the heat intensity remains extremely high. Citizens have been urged to stay well-hydrated, wear loose and light-coloured clothing, and cover their heads with caps, scarves or umbrellas while going outdoors.

articles.title: Severe heatwave grips part of Gujarat as temperature soar: Orange alert for Rajkot & Surendranagar, Yellow ... - Bhaskar English
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat") Gujarat
articles.full_text:
- Gujarat Heatwave: Rajkot & Surendranagar Orange Alert | 9 Cities Yellow Alert
Severe heatwave grips part of Gujarat as temperature soar:Orange alert for Rajkot & Surendranagar, Yellow alert in 9 Cities; people distressed
- Copy link
Since the beginning of the summers in Gujarat it seems like the Sun God has become fierce. The temperature continues to rise in several parts of the state shattering old records. Due to these conditions, the meteorological department has predicted heatwave in many districts on March 12.
Particularly in Rajkot and Surendranagar of Saurashtra, an orange alert has been declared, where the mercury is expected to cross 42 degrees. Meanwhile, yellow alerts have been issued in 9 districts including Ahmedabad, Surat and Vadodara.
Public life affected due to extreme weather conditions
Due to the effect of Western Disturbance, there is a possibility of a slight decrease of 1-2 degree in temperature in the coming days, but currently public life has been affected due to scorching heat and hot winds during afternoon hours forcing people to stay indoors.
Rajkot is currently one of the hottest cities in the state
According to Weather Department Director Dr. A.K. Das, “The temperature in the state will continues to rise. The heat wave will persist in Gujarat in the coming days. Heat wave alerts have been issued in many areas of the state, especially in Saurashtra and North Gujarat”.
He added, “Rajkot is currently one of the hottest cities in the state, where the temperature can reach up to 42 degrees today. Coastal areas like Dwarka and Okha will have relatively lower temperatures, but due to high humidity levels, people may experience heat and sweating”.
Temperature will drop by 1 degree if weather becomes cloudy
According to weather expert A.T. Desai, “Due to Western Disturbance forming towards Himachal Pradesh, maximum temperature could decrease by about 0.5 degrees Celsius. From tomorrow, if the weather becomes cloudy, many cities in the state may see a decrease of 1 degree Celsius in maximum temperature”.
Coastal areas experience unbearable humidity 64 percent of Gujarat's area was under the grip of a heat wave on Wednesday. While the 1600-kilometer coastal area experienced unbearable humidity. Due to this heat wave condition, 8 out of the country's 10 hottest cities were from Gujarat. With 41.9 degrees, Rajkot remained the hottest city in the country. While in Diu, with a temperature of 41.3 degrees, the heat broke a 100-year record.
Vadodara breaks 7-year record with 41.6 degree temperature After Rajkot, Vadodara became the second hottest city with 41.6 degrees and Ahmedabad was third with 41.4 degrees. In the last 75 years, Veraval recorded its fifth highest temperature in March at 40.6 degrees. Vadodara also broke a 7-year record with 41.6 degrees temperature. Due to heatwave in 21 out of 33 districts of the state, 13 cities recorded temperatures more than 5 degrees above normal, creating this situation. Normally, the daytime temperature in the state on March 11 should be between 28.1 to 35.5 degrees. In contrast, 9 cities recorded temperatures above 40 degrees.
Municipal hospitals will keep 10 beds reserved, guidelines for people
It's dangerous when temperature doesn't drop at night after intense heat during day It is considered a dangerous situation when the temperature doesn't decrease at night after intense heat during the day. This has happened in previous years.
Heat started being felt at night from March
When high pressure forms in the atmosphere, hot winds from the lower levels come directly towards the ground. Through this process, when the sky remains clear, sunrays falling directly on the ground suddenly raise the temperature. Cricket grounds, roads and large buildings absorb heat, which causes cities to experience more heat than surrounding rural areas. With a 5.2 degree increase in minimum temperature, heat started being felt at night from March.
Like every year, Municipality will provide ORS water under Heat Action Plan
Following the orange alert i.e. severe heatwave forecast in the city on Wednesday, the Municipality has announced its old heat action plan. 350 water points have been prepared in the city. Municipality will provide ORS at 1000 locations. Last year, about 20 lakh ORS packets were distributed.
These 4 reasons caused increase in temperature
What is Loo or Heatwave?
According to IMD (India Meteorological Department), when the maximum temperature reaches 40 degrees Celsius in plains and 30 degrees Celsius in hilly areas, hot winds start blowing. If the temperature reaches 47 degrees Celsius in any area, it is considered a dangerous category of heatwave.
What causes heat stroke?
Our body's normal temperature is 37 degrees Celsius. However, during summer the surrounding temperature reaches up to 40 to 45 degrees. In such conditions, when we go out in intense sunlight or hot air, the surrounding environment increases our body temperature. When our body temperature rises above the normal body temperature of 37 degrees Celsius, this condition is called heat stroke.
What kind of home remedies can be used in case of heat stroke?
In case of heat stroke, first it is necessary to control the increased body temperature and remove dehydration. In such situations, these remedies can be adopted. Such as-
If a person is suffering from mental problems such as stuttering, fainting or mental tremors, this is a condition of dangerous level heat stroke. In such a situation, the patient should be taken to the hospital immediately. Because there is a risk of damage to body organs in this. The person should not treat themselves in any way. Because in such a situation, even a little carelessness can cause the death of the patient.
What precautions should be taken to avoid heat stroke?
If you experience any kind of problem, contact a doctor immediately.
What kind of diet should one have to prevent heat stroke?
According to medical experts, eat something before going out to prevent heat stroke. Grandparents say that one should never leave home on an empty stomach, there is science behind it. When you go out on an empty stomach, you get tired quickly and lose electrolytes, which causes dehydration. You can drink coconut water to prevent heat and sunstroke.
Additionally, you should not drink cold water immediately after coming from the sun or after excessive sweating. In summer, mix lemon and salt in water and drink it two-three times a day. This reduces the risk of heat stroke.
For those who are troubled by heat stroke, make a paste by grinding barley flour and onion and apply it on the body, it will give them a lot of relief. After coming out from the sun, mixing some onion juice with honey and licking it reduces the risk of heat stroke.
Eating jaggery after meals in the summer season also reduces the risk of heat stroke. In summer, one should take light food and include curd in the diet.

articles.title: અમદાવાદમાં ગરમીનો પ્રકોપ: શહેરના આ 70 સિગ્નલ બપોરે બંધ રહેશે, આજે રાજ્યના 11 જિલ્લામાં હીટવેવનું એલર્ટ - ETV Bharat
articles.search_term: ("ભારે ગરમી" OR "આકરી ગરમી" OR લૂ OR હીટવેવ OR "ઉષ્ણ મોજું" OR "ગરમીનો પ્રકોપ" OR તાપ OR "આકરો તાપ" OR "અસહ્ય ગરમી" OR "ભયાનક ગરમી") Gujarat
articles.full_text:
અમદાવાદમાં ગરમીનો પ્રકોપ: શહેરના આ 70 સિગ્નલ બપોરે બંધ રહેશે, આજે રાજ્યના 11 જિલ્લામાં હીટવેવનું એલર્ટ
અમદાવાદ શહેરમાં પણ ગરમીનો પારો 41 ડિગ્રી સુધી પહોંચી જતા ટ્રાફિક સિગ્નલ બપોરના સમયે બંધ રાખવાનો નિર્ણય લેવામાં આવ્યો છે.
અમદાવાદ: ગુજરાતમાં ઉનાળાની શરૂઆતમાં જ આકરી ગરમી પડી રહી છે. બપોરના સમયે આકાશમાંથી અગનગોળા વરસતા હોય તેવી સ્થિતિ સર્જાય છે. માર્ચ મહિનામાં જ અકળાવનારી ગરમીના કારણે હવામાન વિભાગે ઘણા જિલ્લાઓમાં ગરમીનું ઓરેન્જ અને રેડ એલર્ટ પણ જાહેર કર્યું છે. ત્યારે અમદાવાદ શહેરમાં પણ ગરમીનો પારો 41 ડિગ્રી સુધી પહોંચી જતા ટ્રાફિક સિગ્નલ બપોરના સમયે બંધ રાખવાનો નિર્ણય લેવામાં આવ્યો છે.
શહેરના 70 ટ્રાફિક સિગ્નલ બપોરે બંધ
અમદાવાદ શહેરમાં સવારે અને રાતના સમયે ભારે ટ્રાફિકથી ધમધમતા 70 જેટલા સિગ્નલ બપોરે 12 વાગ્યાથી સાંજે 4 વાગ્યા સુધી બંધ રાખવાનો નિર્ણય કરવામાં આવ્યો છે. શહેરના નવરંગપુરા, કાલુપુર સર્કલ, સ્વસ્તિક ચાર રસ્તા, ઈન્કમટેક્સ સર્કલ, વિજય ચાર રસ્તા, કોમર્સ છ રસ્તા, સ્ટેડિયમ સર્કલ, ખોડિયારનગર ચાર રસ્તા, રખિયાલ ચાર રસ્તા, ફુટબોલ ગ્રાઉન્ડ ચાર રસ્તા, કાગડપીઠ ટી, ચાંદખેડા ચાર રસ્તા, ગોદરેજ ગાર્ડન સિટી, નહેરુબ્રિજ ચાર રસ્તા, સાણંદ સર્કલ સહિતના સ્થળો પર બપોરેના સમયે સિગ્નલ ચાલુ રાખવામાં નહીં આવે.
તમામ 70 સ્થાનોનું લિસ્ટ
રાજકોટ બુધવારે સૌથી ગરમ શહેર રહ્યું
ગુજરાતમાં આજે પણ સુરેન્દ્રનગર અને રાજકોટમાં ગરમીનું ઓરેન્જ એલર્ટ જાહેર કરવામાં આવ્યું છે. જ્યારે વડોદરા, સુરત, અમરેલી, જુનાગઢ, પોરબંદર,મોરબી, પાટણ, બનાસકાંઠા અને કચ્છમાં યેલો એલર્ટ જાહેર કરવામાં આવ્યું છે. બુધવારે રાજકોટમાં 41.9 ડિગ્રી તાપમાન નોંધાયું હતું. અમદાવાદ શહેરમાં પણ 41.4 ડિગ્રી તાપમાન નોંધાયું હતું. આ ઉપરાંત સુરતમાં 41 ડિગ્રી, વડોદરામાં 41.6 ડિગ્રી તાપમાન રહ્યું હતું.
🔸 12થી 14 માર્ચ, 2026 દરમિયાન ગુજરાત સહિત અનેક રાજ્યોમાં કમોસમી વરસાદ, વાવાઝોડા અને હીટવેવને લઈને હવામાન વિભાગે એલર્ટ જાહેર કર્યું
— DD News Gujarati (@DDNewsGujarati) March 12, 2026
🔸 કચ્છ-સૌરાષ્ટ્ર સહિતના અનેક વિસ્તારોમાં હીટવેવની આગાહી
🔸 અરૂણાચલ પ્રદેશમાં આવતીકાલે ભારે વરસાદની શક્યતા
🔸 આગામી બે દિવસમાં હિમાચલ પ્રદેશ,… pic.twitter.com/F6aSqbY602
હવામાન વિભાગની વોર્નિંગ
- હવામાન વિભાગે 11 થી 13 માર્ચ સુધી ગુજરાતમાં તીવ્ર ગરમી પડવાની આગાહી કરી છે અને ત્યારબાદ તેમાં ઘટાડો થવાની શક્યતા છે.
- 12 થી 14 માર્ચ સુધી અરુણાચલ પ્રદેશમાં અને 13 થી 15 માર્ચ સુધી આસામ અને મેઘાલયમાં અલગ અલગ સ્થળોએ ભારે વરસાદ થવાની શક્યતા છે.
- 11 અને 12 માર્ચે હિમાચલ પ્રદેશમાં અલગ અલગ સ્થળોએ વાવાઝોડું, કરા સાથે વીજળી અને જોરદાર પવન ફૂંકાય તેવી શક્યતા છે.
આ પણ વાંચો:
